name: Boudin Du Pays (Blood Pudding)
recipeInstructions:
SAUCE A BOUDIN: When slaughtering a pig, collect the fresh blood. Immediately add salt and stir to prevent coagulation. Cut the fresh pork, lung, heart, and neck into large pieces. Place the meat in a large pot with enough water to cover it. Add the salt and 3 chopped onions. Simmer on medium heat for 3 hours. Remove the meat from the cooking liquid and let it cool. Cut or grind the meat into small pieces. Add the meat to the cooking liquid with the remaining onions, pepper, and spices. Bring the mixture to a boil and slowly add the blood through a sieve, stirring constantly. Add the flour mixed with water. Simmer on low heat for about 1 hour, stirring frequently. This sauce can be warmed in a skillet before serving.
BOUDIN DES BRANCHES (BLOOD PUDDING SAUSAGES): To make blood pudding sausages, prepare the blood pudding sauce without simmering for the last half hour. Clean small intestines and cut them into 20-inch pieces, tying one end. Fill the intestine with the sauce until three-quarters full, pressing out air, then tie the other end. Cook the branches in boiling water for 45 to 1 hour.
